description: This figure presents a schematic illustration of the main crystallographic texture components identified in Fe-30Mn-11Al-1.2C low-density steel during cold rolling, displayed as three characteristic sections of the orientation distribution function (ODF) at constant φ2 angles of 0°, 45°, and 65°. The left panel (φ2 = 0°) depicts the α-fiber trajectory running from Goss (G) orientation through Brass (B) to Rotated Goss (RtG), alongside the Cube (C) component located at the top-left corner. The central panel (φ2 = 45°) illustrates the τ-fiber (horizontal) and γ-fiber (vertical), highlighting key deformation components including Copper (Cu), Copper Twin (CuT), and intermediate positions marked E and F. The right panel (φ2 = 65°) remains largely blank with minimal labeling (SO). Various geometric symbols represent distinct ideal orientations: squares denote Cube, diamonds indicate Goss and Brass, hexagons represent Rotated Goss, while triangles mark Copper and related orientations. This diagram establishes the reference framework for analyzing texture evolution, particularly the transition from copper-type to brass-type texture during progressive cold rolling, as defined in Table 1.
